brand.name: Live News
brand.mission: Make current coverage readable, low-clutter, source-respectful, and easy to verify through original source links.

voice.plainLanguageRule: Use words a reader can understand quickly without flattening the facts.
voice.sourceRespectRule: Use Live News wording, keep attribution visible, and never replace the original publisher's full reporting.
voice.sensitivityRule: Use calm, neutral language for crime, death, injury, politics, legal claims, children, health, disasters, and active alerts.
voice.publicSafetyRule: Public safety language is allowed only when the article clearly supports an advisory, alert, evacuation, closure, recall, warning, missing person notice, disaster, or source-backed safety instruction.

title.goal: Name the confirmed event without hype or copied publisher structure.
rules: Lead with the actual person, group, place, decision, event, or result.
Avoid default labels like Top Story or Breaking unless the source context explicitly supports that framing.
Do not copy the publisher headline exactly.
Keep titles compact enough for cards and search results.

description.goal: Explain what happened and why it matters in one or two source-faithful sentences.
rules: Use one specific confirmed detail from the article context.
Avoid vague phrases like this article discusses or latest update on this topic.
Do not invent background, public reaction, or next steps.
If context is weak, return needs_more_context instead of public filler.

dek.goal: Give the reader a fast subheadline that adds context beyond the title.
rules: Include a concrete source-backed detail not already obvious from the title.
Keep the tone calm and explanatory.
Do not use internal workflow terms.

summary.goal: Summarize the confirmed situation in plain language without copying RSS descriptions.
rules: Prefer what happened first, then who is affected or why it matters.
Keep the summary short enough for cards.
Vary rhythm across nearby cards.
Block generic fallback text from public display.

whyItMatters.goal: Explain reader relevance without exaggerating impact.
rules: Tie importance to confirmed people, places, companies, teams, policies, audiences, or services.
Avoid broad claims like may affect everyone unless the source context proves it.
Do not force public-safety framing.

homepageCard.goal: Help readers choose quickly without feeling pushed.
rules: Make the title and summary understandable without opening the source.
Keep labels quiet and useful.
Avoid robotic badges or repeated template language.

seoMetadata.goal: Describe the page accurately for search without keyword stuffing.
rules: Use the exact story/topic and Live News brand naturally.
Keep meta descriptions specific and source-respectful.
Do not include external publisher URLs in sitemap metadata.
Avoid generic page filler.

socialCaption.goal: Create human-readable Facebook and Instagram captions that drive to exact Live News story pages.
rules: Use a useful lead, one context sentence, source-safe attribution, and the exact story URL when required.
Generate platform-specific variants instead of reusing the same caption.
Do not copy publisher wording or public comments.
Do not include internal workflow language.

socialCardText.goal: Make image/card text scannable, specific, and aligned with the article.
rules: Use short card titles that match the story.
Use subtitles only when they add source-backed context.
Alt text should describe the card/story without inventing image details.

weakFallbackPatterns: Read the original source for the full report.
A clear look at the latest source-linked details

preferredWritingShapes.notes: Best when the article has a clear event and a confirmed reader relevance angle.

preferredWritingShapes.shape: {{personOrGroup}} is involved in {{mainEvent}}, with the story focusing on {{readerAngle}}.
preferredWritingShapes.notes: Best for people, teams, companies, agencies, artists, officials, and local groups.
Best when the story belongs to a continuing topic or multi-day arc.
Use only when the main question is specific, source-backed, and not generic.
Best for games, awards, premieres, concerts, TV moments, elections, hearings, and public events.

local.focus: place, residents, daily impact, public services, local decisions
local.avoid: turning every local story into public safety

national.focus: public decisions, policy, accountability, communities affected
national.avoid: partisan hype or unsupported national impact

international.focus: country, conflict, diplomacy, policy, global impact
international.avoid: stereotypes, broad cultural assumptions, certainty beyond the source

business.focus: company, market, money, workers, consumers, investors

technology.focus: product, platform, users, privacy, tools, regulation

sports.focus: team, result, player, injury, matchup, next game

entertainment.focus: person, release, audience, event, culture moment, TV, movies, music
entertainment.avoid: gossip presented as fact or copied tabloid phrasing
